name: Monstera
description: The Monstera, or Swiss Cheese Plant, is famous for its dramatic split leaves. A showstopper in any room, it's forgiving for beginners and grows fast with the right light.
tips: Wipe leaves monthly to keep them dust-free and glossy.
Use a moss pole to encourage upward growth.
Yellow leaves usually mean overwatering — let soil dry between waterings.

name: Snake Plant
description: One of the most resilient houseplants around. The Snake Plant thrives on neglect, tolerates low light, and purifies the air while you sleep by releasing oxygen at night.
tips: Overwatering is the #1 killer — always let soil fully dry out.
Great for bedrooms due to its night-time oxygen production.
Avoid direct harsh sunlight which can scorch the leaves.
Wipe leaves with a damp cloth to remove dust.

name: Pothos
description: Pothos is the ultimate starter plant. Its trailing vines look beautiful on shelves and in hanging baskets. It adapts to almost any light condition and tells you when it's thirsty by drooping slightly.
tips: Let vines trail or train them up a support for a climbing look.
Propagate easily in water — just snip below a node.
Yellowing leaves = overwatering. Brown tips = underwatering or low humidity.
Toxic to pets — keep out of reach of dogs and cats.

name: Peace Lily
description: The Peace Lily is one of the few houseplants that blooms beautifully even in low light. Its elegant white flowers and lush dark green leaves make it a classic interior plant.
tips: It will dramatically droop when thirsty — a helpful built-in reminder.
Mist leaves regularly or place on a pebble tray for humidity.
Toxic to pets and children — handle with care.

name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
description: The superstar of interior design, the Fiddle Leaf Fig commands attention with its large, violin-shaped leaves. It rewards patience and consistency with stunning growth.
tips: Hates being moved — find its happy spot and leave it there.
Brown spots on edges = underwatering. Brown spots in the middle = root rot.
Dust leaves regularly — dirty leaves block photosynthesis.

name: ZZ Plant
description: The ZZ Plant stores water in its rhizomes, making it incredibly drought-tolerant. Its waxy, deep green leaves stay glossy with almost no effort, perfect for busy plant parents.
tips: One of the most forgiving houseplants — great for forgetful waterers.
Never let it sit in water — root rot sets in quickly.
Grows slowly but steadily; don't rush it.
Toxic if ingested — wash hands after handling.

name: Spider Plant
description: Spider Plants are cheerful, fast-growing, and incredibly easy to propagate — they produce little 'spiderettes' you can share with friends.
tips: Hang in a basket to show off trailing spiderettes.
Root the spiderettes in water or soil to make new plants.
Brown tips are usually caused by fluoride in tap water.

name: Aloe Vera
description: Aloe Vera is both beautiful and useful. Its gel-filled leaves soothe burns and skin irritation.
tips: Allow soil to dry completely between waterings.
Use aloe gel for minor burns and dry skin.

name: Rubber Plant
description: With its large glossy leaves, the Rubber Plant makes a bold statement indoors.

name: Boston Fern
description: Boston Ferns are lush, feathery, and dramatic hanging plants.
tips: Never let soil dry out completely.

name: Lavender
description: Lavender fills spaces with calming fragrance and purple blooms.

name: Basil
description: Fresh basil grows quickly in warmth and sunlight.

name: Chinese Evergreen
description: Chinese Evergreens are adaptable and beautifully variegated.

name: Bird of Paradise
description: Bird of Paradise is a bold tropical plant with large architectural leaves.
tips: Allow top soil to dry slightly.

name: Cactus
description: Cacti are sculptural, resilient, and thrive in bright sunny spaces.

name: Calathea
description: Calatheas are famous for their stunning patterned leaves that move throughout the day like praying hands.
tips: Use filtered water to avoid brown leaf edges.

name: Jade Plant
description: Jade Plants symbolize prosperity and are loved for their thick glossy leaves and tree-like appearance.

name: Orchid
description: Orchids produce exotic long-lasting blooms and elevate any indoor space beautifully.
tips: Do not let roots sit in water.

name: Areca Palm
description: Areca Palms create a lush tropical atmosphere indoors and are excellent natural humidifiers.

name: Chamomile
description: Chamomile is cherished for its calming flowers commonly used in soothing herbal teas.

name: Philodendron
description: Philodendrons are adaptable trailing plants with lush heart-shaped leaves.
tips: Allow top soil to dry slightly.

name: Croton
description: Crotons are known for their intensely colorful leaves in shades of red, orange, yellow, and green.
tips: Needs bright light for vivid colors.

name: Bamboo
description: Lucky Bamboo is popular in homes and offices for its minimal care needs and symbolic positivity.

name: Thyme
description: Thyme is a fragrant herb used widely in cooking and herbal remedies.

name: Peperomia
description: Peperomias are compact charming plants with thick leaves and easy-care nature.
